title: arXiv Table 5 -- exact Gillespie SSA cost, published real-world models
revision: 2026-07-18h: removed the BNGL vilar_circadian (a modified delta_R=0.05 variant); kept the SBML BIOMD35 (published delta_R=0.2). Vilar now SBML-only. Set now 8 BNGL + 6 SBML.
selection_rule: Every model is (1) published and (2) simulated with exact Gillespie SSA IN ITS SOURCE STUDY, at (3) the published time horizon. The three Lin-2019 systems (tcr/erk/prion) are the exact-SSA baseline here and the PSA approximation in Table 7, by design.

provenance_legend.confirmed: source study demonstrably used SSA; reference solid.
provenance_legend.verify: originally-SSA is probable but Bill should confirm the reference / that the source study used SSA.

engine_columns.sbml_note: All three SBML columns are EXACT SSA (bngsim SSA, RoadRunner gillespie, COPASI SSA). Every engine is run on every model; where an engine cannot produce a valid trajectory, its cell is marked N/A with a footnote. Known gap: RoadRunner-gillespie silently does not fire time-triggered events (860/862/864), so those RR cells are expected N/A.

notes: BNGL rows are exact SSA on the pre-generated .net (both engines run the SAME network).
BNGL Lin-2019 nets (tcr/erk/prion) were regenerated from RuleHub Published/Lin2019/*.bngl with BNG 2.9.3; horizons are the Lin-2019 'exact' SSA actions.
Timings are NOT recorded here -- this file defines the CORPUS (models + horizons + provenance). A timing run is a separate step.

SBML inclusion rule: molecule-count encodings ONLY (initialAmount / hasOnlySubstanceUnits=true). Concentration-unit SBML (hasOnlySubstanceUnits=false) is an ODE encoding -- exact SSA on it is ill-posed. Dropped for this reason: Smith2013/474 (ROS went negative) and Karapetyan/586+587.

bngl.name: tcr_signaling
bngl.reference: Lipniacki T, Hat B, Faeder JR, Hlavacek WS (2008) J Theor Biol 254:110-122. Formulation + horizon: Lin YT, Feng S, Hlavacek WS (2019) J Chem Phys 150:244101.
caveats: Regenerated from RuleHub Lin-2019 TCR_model.bngl at the Lin-2019 exact-SSA horizon t_end=10000.

bngl.name: erk_activation
bngl.reference: Kochanczyk M et al. (2017) Sci Rep 7:38244. Formulation + horizon: Lin et al. (2019).
caveats: Regenerated from RuleHub Lin-2019 ERK_model.bngl at t_end=8640 (Lin-2019 exact-SSA action).
EXACT SSA IS THE SLOW ANCHOR: populations up to 3e6 (set via *_tot params) -> ~6e8 events/replicate (~77 s/rep in bng_parity). Feasible; budget for it.
Confirm the Kochanczyk 2017 reference and SSA origin.

bngl.name: prion_aggregation
bngl.reference: Rubenstein et al. (2007), nucleated polymerization. Formulation + horizon: Lin et al. (2019).
caveats: Regenerated from RuleHub Lin-2019 prion_model.bngl at t_end=300, n_steps=30000 (Lin-2019 exact-SSA action; generate_network uses max_stoich PrP=>120).
Confirm the Rubenstein 2007 reference and SSA origin.

bngl.name: gene_expr_3stage
bngl.reference: Shahrezaei V, Swain PS (2008) Analytical distributions for stochastic gene expression. PNAS 105:17256-17261.
caveats: Three-stage single-gene model: DNA(promoter on/off) -> mRNA -> Protein. Small network, very long horizon (t_end=2e8) drives the event count.
DISTINCT from gene_expression (Munsky) -- single-gene protein cascade vs multi-gene mRNA telegraph.

bngl.name: gene_expression
bngl.reference: Munsky B, Neuert G, van Oudenaarden A (2012) Science 336:183-187. BNGL encoding: Chylek LA, Harris LA, Faeder JR, Hlavacek WS (2015) Phys Biol 12:045007, Fig 9.
caveats: Munsky Fig-9 model: 1 constitutive gene + 3 two-state regulated genes at equal mean but distinct transcript distributions (mRNA-level telegraph).
BNGL is an illustrative re-implementation from the Chylek 2015 review; the model originates in Munsky 2012. Both used SSA.

bngl.name: mckane_predator_prey
bngl.reference: McKane AJ, Newman TJ (2005) Predator-prey cycles from resonant amplification of demographic stochasticity. Phys Rev Lett 94:218102.
caveats: DEMOGRAPHIC (ecological) stochasticity -- individuals, not molecules. It is exact SSA of a Markov jump process, but ecological rather than biochemical; keep or prune depending on table scope.
IC n=m=800 per the header note; network generated here with BNG 2.9.3 (3 sp / 5 rx). Source BNGL is Bill's encoding of the McKane-Newman reactions.

bngl.name: gene_bursts
bngl.reference: Lin YT, Doering CR (2016) Gene expression dynamics with stochastic bursts: construction and exact results for a coarse-grained model. Phys Rev E 93:022409.
caveats: Coarse-grained bursty gene expression (mRNA + protein). Thematically near gene_expr_3stage / gene_expression but a distinct model.

ICs BAKED: seed species set to the ODE-equilibrated steady state (mRNA=0, Protein=467) so exact SSA measures the intended post-equilibration regime. The source runs an ODE equilibration (t_end=360000) then SSA; from bare mRNA=0/Protein=0 ICs the model is near-inert (basal transcription only).

bngl.name: samoilov_futile_cycle
bngl.reference: Samoilov M, Plyasunov S, Arkin AP (2005) Stochastic amplification and signaling in enzymatic futile cycles through noise-induced bistability and oscillations. PNAS 102:2310-2315.
caveats: Enzymatic futile cycle with noise-induced bistability (Arkin group). Very short horizon (t_end=0.0018), small network (6 sp / 6 rx).
BNGL is a from_antimony conversion (mass-action, molecule counts) -- verify parameters match Samoilov 2005 before final use.

sbml.title: Proctor2017 - microRNAs in osteoarthritis (positive feedforward, incoherent)
sbml.reference: Proctor CJ, Smith GR (2017) role of microRNAs in osteoarthritis -- confirm citation.
caveats: Time-triggered events: RoadRunner-gillespie silently does not fire them -> RR cell N/A. bngsim and COPASI both run it. Provenance strong (Proctor group).

sbml.title: Proctor2017 - microRNAs in osteoarthritis (positive feedback)
sbml.reference: Proctor CJ, Smith GR (2017) -- confirm citation.
caveats: Time-triggered events: RoadRunner-gillespie does not fire them -> RR cell N/A. bngsim and COPASI run it. Provenance strong (Proctor group).

sbml.title: Proctor2017 - microRNAs in osteoarthritis (negative feedback)
sbml.reference: Proctor CJ, Smith GR (2017) -- confirm citation.
caveats: Time-triggered events: RoadRunner-gillespie does not fire them -> RR cell N/A. bngsim and COPASI run it. Provenance strong (Proctor group).

sbml.title: Proctor2011 - protein homeostasis (high stress)
sbml.reference: Proctor CJ, Lorimer IA (2011) protein homeostasis (Hsp70/Hsp90) -- confirm citation.
caveats: No time-triggered events -- all three engines run it (it is just slow). Run to the FULL horizon (feasible -- the source study ran the full simulation); the parity screen flagged partial_horizon only because of its short wall cap.
CONFIRM the paper's real horizon: the inherited figure-SED-ML value t_end=10 looks short for a proteostasis time course.

sbml.title: Besozzi2012 - oscillatory regimes in the Ras/cAMP/PKA pathway (S. cerevisiae)
sbml.reference: Besozzi D, Cazzaniga P, Pescini D, Mauri G, Colombo S, Martegani E (2012) EURASIP J Bioinform Syst Biol 2012:10. PMID 22818197.
caveats: Model NOTES state it explicitly: 'defined according to the stochastic formulation of chemical kinetics [Gillespie 1977] ... performing stochastic simulations'. Non-Proctor group (Milano-Bicocca stochastic-simulation lab). Adds a signaling-oscillator dimension.
No events/rules -> all three engines run it. DIFF in the screen was partial_horizon (wall cap); run to the full paper horizon.

sbml.title: Vilar2002 - circadian oscillator (molecule-count SBML)
sbml.reference: Vilar JMG, Kueh HY, Barkai N, Leibler S (2002) Mechanisms of noise-resistance in genetic oscillators. PNAS 99:5988-5992.
caveats: Molecule-count encoding (hasOnlySubstanceUnits=true for all 10 species; DA=DR=1, rest 0) -- passes the SSA molecule-count rule; runs clean (no negative species).

The BNGL twin (vilar_circadian) was REMOVED: it differed in exactly one rate constant -- repressor degradation delta_R = 0.05 (written 0.2/4 in the RuleHub source) vs the published Vilar-2002 value 0.2 used here. All 14 other rate constants and the 16-reaction network are identical. This SBML is the faithful encoding.
Non-Proctor -- one of two non-Proctor SBML rows (with Besozzi/478).
